free_text: -A STUDY OF THE TEMPERATURE DEPENDENCE OF THE
.URANIUM DIOXIDE SAMPLES PREPARED AT HARWELL BY
SCINTERING AT 1850 DEGK, FOLLOWED BY SLOW COOLING
AND MACHINING TO CORRECT SIZE. SEVERAL SAMPLES
WERE USED, ALL 20 MM DIAMETER, THICKNESS OF 5, 10
AND 20 MM. ISOTOPIC COMPOSITION OF 99.678 PC U-238
.SAMPLES WERE HEATED IN A TUNGSTEN TUBE OF 0.5MM
WALL THICKNESS, ABOUT 400 MM LONG, THROUGH WHICH
A HIGH CURRENT OF UP TO 2 KA WAS PASSED FOR HEATING.
.THE NEUTRON BEAM PASSED THROUGH 2MM THICK QUARTZ
WINDOWS COAXIAL WITH THE HEATER TUBE.
TIME OF FLIGHT, FLIGHT PATH 35 METERS,
COLLIMATED BEAM IN EVACUATED FLIGHT PATH.
SCINTILLATOR BASED ON A 25 MM THICK, 76 MM
CONTIANING 8.7 PERCENT BY WEIGHT OF LITHIUM
ARE GIVEN FOR VARIOUS SAMPLE THICKNESSES AND SAMPLE
AND UNCERTAINTY IN BACKGROUND DUE TO INCORRECT
EXTRAPOLATION OF THE FILTERED BACKGROUND TO ZERO
RATIO OF AVERAGE TRANSMISSIONS WITH TEMPERATURES
